name: ambient-minicpm-v-vision
description: Local MiniCPM-V visual-understanding provider wrapper for Ambient screenshot and image evidence.

env.name: AMBIENT_MINICPM_V_LLAMA_SERVER
env.description: Optional approved llama-server command or absolute path. Desktop can bind a user-managed binary, install a user-approved pinned archive, or use the default managed macOS arm64/Linux x64 runtime download after archive/binary checksum verification. The manifest keeps Windows default download disabled and records macOS signing/Gatekeeper facts for the managed copy.

env.name: AMBIENT_MINICPM_V_MODEL
env.description: Optional llama.cpp -hf model ref. Defaults to openbmb/MiniCPM-V-4_5-gguf:q4_k_m.

env.name: AMBIENT_MINICPM_V_EXPERIMENTAL_MODEL
env.description: Optional comparison model ref. Defaults to openbmb/MiniCPM-V-4.6-gguf:q4_k_m.

env.name: AMBIENT_MINICPM_V_STATE_DIR
env.description: Optional workspace-local runtime state directory. Defaults to .ambient/minicpm-v.

env.name: AMBIENT_MINICPM_V_PORT
env.description: Optional local llama-server port. Defaults to 39217.

env.name: AMBIENT_MINICPM_V_ENDPOINT
env.description: Optional approved local OpenAI-compatible MiniCPM endpoint URL such as http://127.0.0.1:39217. Remote endpoints are intentionally rejected by the wrapper until the hosted-endpoint security review covers allowed hosts, consent, media privacy, secrets, redaction, retention, egress controls, and UI copy.

minicpm_vision_status.description: Report MiniCPM-V runtime, model, local endpoint, and install readiness without starting the server.

minicpm_vision_verify_runtime_manifest.description: Validate the blocked MiniCPM-V llama-server release manifest/checksum contract without downloading or executing runtime artifacts.

minicpm_vision_start.description: Start a local llama-server MiniCPM-V daemon on 127.0.0.1 using the default 4.5 Q4_K_M model unless overridden.

minicpm_vision_stop.description: Stop the workspace-local MiniCPM-V llama-server daemon started by this package.

minicpm_vision_analyze.description: Analyze one bounded local image, or a two-image current/reference comparison, through the MiniCPM-V endpoint and write full JSON artifacts.
